reviews.review_text:
This Youtube video I recorded over a decade ago pretty much sums up how I feel about this game. 
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4WlFls2QVfY
Got this game to play with friends, then proceeded to lead a resistance group on an island off the coast of Greece. Will recommend.
Excellent game, great realism, however the UI is hard to understand but you get used to it.

I'm still new to ARMA so I haven't explored everything; but what's here and the communities you can find (mainly on ARMA 3 unit subreddit or the like) makes this the best game in my library. 
Don't get me started on the mods. Trying to summarise them is like trying to summarise every atom in the milky way.

In earlier years, I would have whole-heartedly recommended this game. But- with the dwindling servers, lower player count, limits, etc., I can no longer recommend this game. It breaks my heart, but this title is unofficially deader than a carp on the deck that stopped flopping hours ago.
This game feels like being offered a blowjob and receiving exclusively teeth.

arma 3 is a game for a very specialized audience
military nerds, mostly

i am exactly that

Best game ever, friends love it, melts your PC, worth every penny, even ending a 5 year friendship in this game didn't make me hate it

Got a new graphics card with Arma IN MIND, worth every penny Bohemia stabs out of your pockets

if u wanna play single player/co-op or antistasi this is the way to go but if you want to play multiplayer pvp go buy reforger. this games pvp is basically a more tactical call of duty or battlefield but arma reforgers confilct game mode is just the war simulator you might be looking for
The idea that after all this time they're doing a subscription pass is disappointing to say the least. Just a cash grab. Don't buy it.

bohemia should not be introducing passes for a 13 year old game. 
it is an obvious attempt to groom the community to accept subscription models. 

i have >1.5k hours in this game, plenty of sweet memories, but i cannot accept these predatory tactics towards the community -- especially when they are the only reasons for arma 3's continued success in the first place.

RELEASE DATE:
Sep 12, 2013

The game is old and is only kept alive by a devoted modding community. Most of the DLC is also buggy trash. 

So getting rid of the base game to bundle it with unwanted DLC, pushing a scummy subscription model for a 13 year old game, and still so much that is unstable and broken about this game... BI is cracked man. Cracked.
I really wanted to enjoy this game after seeing youtubers showing off some really cool gameplay, but the game is so woefully unapproachable I could never get into it.
At first it seems like a great game and then you get lost just wandering around because none of it works anything like any other game.  Geez....a tutorial might help.

my pc runs most games at 160+ fps with no problems however this game runs at 7 fps normally and 19 fps if i optimize everything. there is also nealr zero active servers if you live in the GTA. buy arma reforger instead
Good sim. Not for your CS or COD lovers. Definitely way more simulation oriented. However really good game and not a resource hog.

This game is very well put together. Has a long history and would like to see a whole rework one day, with smarter AI, more Commanding option. And more detail scripted and a more details in general. Other then that great game by far.

The best military sandbox game to ever exist. If you can think of a movie, game or comic chances are arma has a mod for it somewhere. Arma 3 has always been a cpu intensive game so beware those with older computers. Slide shows can occur with tons of entities and/or poorly optimized mods being added to the map. 

You can reenact any era of Star Wars, 40k, Starship Troopers or Halo 3 with the right group/community. It's a blast. 
Or stick to vanilla and enjoy the plethora of fun game modes that have been created over the years. Great game, great time sink and fun times with fun ppl. Pick it up immediately, especially if its on sale.
very realistic and tuff and the tuffest part is the editor mode

Bought it years ago, still playing. The vanilla game is great, but the mods take it to another level. Some of my best gaming memories came from Arma 3 operations with friends. Worth every penny.

Very good game, a bit expensive. The only thing is you need to get used to the unusal controls. Whether you like call of duty or DCS or just realism, i find this game fun as there is lots of mods. P.S Runs well with my ultrawide monitor 1440p. I can also fit this game in my 1tb NVME even with games like DCS installed

Arma 3 is a good tactical shooter that can be fun either alone or with friends. Additionally, with mods you can do many things like turn it into a WW2, Cold War, HALO, or War Hammer 40K game and more. The controls are difficult to learn.

Arma 3 is definitely where it's at, and while I have not dived into multiplayer online much yet, I have played with the editor and with Zeus, and I have to say it is better than 2's system by a landslide, and the things that were unpleasant about 2 in regards to movement and stance, have been fixed wonderfully. Optimization is still apparently not BI's favorite thing, but some community workshop mods for perf tweaks and dynamic view distance and a few parameters in the ol' command line, and this game runs well on even almost 15 year old machines. Ravage is great, and the DayZ style missions are terrific, in my opinion. (As someone who has over 1k hours on DayZ spread out between Arma2 and the SA version, I play SA on PS so I have a very biased opinion) Either way, with the workshop being flush with content and modules for easier and more fluid creation, this game takes the cake in terms of endless replay value, as well sheer quantity of content. 
Arma3 will probably be played for years to come.
amazing gameplay and plentiful content! Graphics can be a bit spotty but its overall a good game!

game of the year, 10/10

The best military sandbox ever made.

Arma 3 isn't just a shooter—it's a complete military simulation and sandbox. The scale of the maps, realism of the combat, and freedom to approach missions however you want make it unlike any other game. Whether you're playing single-player, cooperative operations with friends, PvP scenarios, or massive modded campaigns, the amount of content is practically endless.

The modding community is incredible and keeps the game fresh even after thousands of hours. From realistic military operations to zombie survival, roleplay servers, and custom campaigns, there's always something new to experience.

The learning curve can be steep, but once everything clicks, no other game delivers the same level of immersion and teamwork. Even after all these years, Arma 3 remains the gold standard for large-scale military simulation.

If you enjoy realism, tactical gameplay, and unlimited replayability, Arma 3 is an easy recommendation. 🚁🎖️💥

Hours played: "Still learning new things after hundreds of hours."

An amazing milsim sandbox.

Arma 3 requires a bit of getting into, but after that it becomes pretty intuitive and simple.
Modding support in this game is on the level of Gmod, basically godtier.

After 450+ hours, I can confidently say this remains one of the most immersive military sandbox experiences ever made.

What keeps me coming back isn't just the gameplay, it's the teamwork, discipline, and roleplay that emerge when a group of players commits to a mission. Whether you're conducting a reconnaissance patrol, coordinating a combined arms assault, or simply trying to get everyone loaded into the right vehicle on time, Arma creates moments that feel earned. Success comes from communication, planning, and trusting the people around you rather than individual heroics.

The community is one of the game's greatest strengths. I've met some outstanding players, leaders, mission makers, and server administrators who dedicate countless hours to creating memorable experiences for others. A special shoutout goes to the modding community, whose work has transformed Arma 3 into far more than a game. From realism overhauls and roleplay frameworks to total conversion mods and custom campaigns, their passion has kept this title thriving for years.

As a dad gamer with limited free time, I appreciate that every session feels like an adventure worth remembering. Few games reward teamwork, patience, and professionalism the way Arma 3 does. If you're looking for a shooter that values cooperation over kill counts and immersion over instant gratification, Arma 3 is an easy recommendation.

Arma 3, especially with the Antistasi mod is such an amazing experience. For a game released in 2013 Arma 3 is still such an unbelievably realistic and good looking game that runs and feels good. However the mods definitely carry the game.

needs a cpu with the highest single core performance you can get but even without that this game is amazing 20fps is totally playable and if you combine it with a 3rd party frame generation app its perfect community content and dlc is worth every GB and penny you spend
So much better than Call of Duty. This is a military game I can get into alot.
the game is fun and good for roleplaying

You ever want to turn a IFV or a Tank into a spacecraft, this is the game for you, just ram another vehicle and boom you started your own space program. (Other than the jank of the game I highly recommend you get this on sale with a bunch of friends)
Great combat simulator, especially with mods and friends.

I love this game and hate it, yet I keep coming back to it anyway. Even though Bohemia Interactive has stopped supporting Arma 3 with new DLCs, modders have practically filled the need for new content. Sure, there are CDLCs, but many of them are mediocre in my opinion, because they don’t add to the game—they basically build a whole new world on the same foundation. That’s cool, but it doesn’t really do it for me.
I’d like to give a special shout-out to the Antistasi Ultimate mod—I’ve literally spent ~700 hours just on this mod alone, at peak playtime, no less.
But honestly, the optimization is lacking; maybe my PC is just a piece of crap, I don’t know. But getting a near-constant 30 FPS on a not-too-crowded map is insane.
I’m really looking forward to the release of Arma 4, and even more so to returning to my favorite Antistasi in its new form.
Cant recommend enough, best military sandbox game. Playing solo is a lot of fun

10/10

buy it if you love tactical shooters or fps

My only regret is that I slept on this game so long. It's a bit obtuse and janky, but its totally forgivable considering how much replayability it has to offer. It's hard to call Arma a game. Arma is a solo/coop PvE-centered milsim platform. It comes with a decent campaign and some individual missions/tutorials showcasing different aspects of the game, but the real value comes from the editor. 

Remember playing with those green/brown toy soldiers as a kid? You would gather with friends, and imagine all sorts of different scenarios. Arma is digital representation of that experience. It's dungeons and dragons but with tanks. 

What makes this even better is 13 years worth of amazing community mods and scenarios available in workshop that can deliver unique experience every session. You want thousand different weapons and even more real life attachments for them? Knock yourself out. You want to become a warlord? Go for it.  You wanna build a zombie apocalypse? you got it. Want to play survival hunting artifacts in the zone? Sure. Want to play PvP instead of PvE? They got that too. 

With such depth comes a bit of complexity. The tutorial in this game is fairly decent, but you will probably have to spend some time watching youtube videos to learn some basics. There is a spreadsheet worth of control mappings you will need to reference until you memorize the essentials. You'll either need to spend some time making scenarios in the editor, or spend time looking for scenarios in workshop (though admittedly the good scenarios are not hard to find). 

This is an older game with crappy optimization. It doesn't know how to use most out of your resources. For me the game runs at 60FPS on Ultra, but my hardware is only ever at 50% utilization. While it's definitely playable, it's fairly easy to turn into a slideshow on more intense scenarios.

As far as DLC subscription thing:  It's amusing to see people who spent $35 total buying up all DLCs on sale throughout the years and putting in master's degree worth of playtime calling developers greedy. This is a 13-year old game that is still receiving updates to this day. Personally I don't see a problem as long there's an option to buy them individually. The subscription is merely an option for some people, and I can see it being a good value for someone who just wants to dip their toes in a trial run to see which ones are worth buying, if at all. The DLCs are decent, but entirely optional. You can always get additional tanks, planes, helicopters, weapons, maps, and scenarios from workshop for free anyways.

[h1]ARMA 3 review[/h1]
[h2]Gameplay[/h2]
Gameplay of ARMA 3 is good, I love the mechanics, the gunplay, tactics and strategies. The NPCs in this game are well-made. Driving in ARMA 3 is also quite interesting for me. Story seems ok, well-made and made with some quality.

[h2]My rating of this game[/h2]

7/10
Why? The issue with ARMA 3 is not the gameplay, quality, and story but the contents of the game. Majority of the missions in ARMA 3 are required to be purchased as a DLC, which I am disappointed in, you have to purchase so many DLCs to play singleplayer for a longer time. I will still recommend this game for audiences who are willing to buy DLCs for additional missions and story.

[h2]Arma 3 Pass? I just shid out my ass...[/h2]

Here I am waiting for Bohemia Interactive (BI) to lower the price of their DLC weapons and vehicles on a 13+ year old game so that I can finish my collection, only to see that some corporate ghoul decided to demand an 'in perpetuity' subscription to use the DLCs we all used to simply buy once and keep forever. KEKW. Credibility nullified overnight. If this is the beginning of the roadmap to Arma 4, count me the f--k out.
